MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Edward Thirkell for and with Majid Farmanfarmaian.
Charles Wolff for and with Mansoor Farmanfarmaian (Trustee).

Mr. Thirkell stated that portions of accounting provided by Trustee were redacted. Mr. Wolff stated they are never satisfied with whatever they give, that the document was not redacted, that was the best they could do with copies, provided original for Mr. Thirkell and his client to review and requested document returned after hearing. Mr. Thirkell stated that they don't have a problem with the accounting, but his client is entitled to back-up documents to accounting and frustrated by lack of information. The Court stated that Trusts are intended to be administered outside of the court, that this trust is not under court supervision, that the settlor's intents govern, and that it is the Trustee's role to protect the interest of the Trust and beneficiaries. Trustee gives update on pending sale: acceptance of 5th offer at $3.9 million with technical offer to fix a water leak, 45 days from 12/20/19 for close of escrow, the presence of asbestos, and expressed his concern about being able to deliver property in condition offer was made before the leak occurred. Mr. Thirkell stated he wants a copy of the offer and acceptance and emphasized his client wants to be kept in the loop, that the Trustee can email his client and the attorney do not even need to get involved. Mr. Wolff stated the Trustee has no interest in dragging his feet, that he tried to speed up the process by accepting an offer less than what they hoped for due to things that come up. The Court orders bi-weekly updates, stating interpersonal conflict needs to be set aside and for free-flow of information between trustee and beneficiaries.

The Third Report and Account of Trustee, and Petition for Approval and Settlement is Taken Under Submission.

The Pre-Trial Conference and Petition to Compel Distribution, etc., are continued to February 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status report on: sale of Jones Street property, bigger picture of petition, ADR, discovery, claims that can be resolved before trial, claims that need hearing, factual issues, witness list and time estimates to be filed by January 24, 2020.

Trustee's Petition for Authorization to Pay Trustees Fees from the Trust currently set for January 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept 204 is also continued to February 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 per oral order of the court.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Edward Thirkell for and with Majid Farmanfarmaian.
Charles Wolff for and with Mansoor Farmanfarmaian.

Mr. Thirkell stated that several issues were raised in the status report, that the concern is that it has taken three years to sell a property in San Francisco, that the largest apartment has not been rented, that it is his understanding that the rental value is about $8,400.00 a month and that it is a loss to the trust. Mr. Thirkell also stated that trust law requires that a Trustee make trust property productive in income, that the Trustee is not otherwise employed, questions if the Trustee is paying himself trustee fees, stated that there has been no accounting and requested that the Court set a date for an accounting. Mr. Wolff suggested setting a hearing to file an accounting within 30-days, stated that the Trustee has not paid himself, that the trust allows a flat fee of $50,000.00 and that Judge Stewart authorized a rate of $75.00 per hour. The Court noted that the comments made in the report regarding the Court are disrespectful, that there are several issues with the property and that having one unit vacant to allow an owner to move in is attractive when selling a property. Mr. Thirkell stated that they are looking for a schedule/more structure. Regarding the Jones Street property, Mr. Wolff stated that they just entered into a third contract and they are hoping that it does not fall through, that they have a closing date for the sale of October 17th, and that if closed, they will be in a position to make distributions to the Beneficiaries by the middle of November. Mr. Thirkell requested a copy of the offer/contract. Mr. Wolff responded "sure" to the request. The per-trial conference and the Petition to Compel Distributions to Beneficiaries, etc. are continued to November 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Wolff's accounting petition is to be given a hearing of November 18, 2019.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jun-17-2019 Continued To Aug-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Wolff for and with Mansoor Farmanfamaian.
Edward Thirkell for and with Majid Farmanfamaian.
Massoud Farmanfamaian appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Mr. Thirkell stated that they asking for suspension or a report as to the marketing of the properties (Jones Street and Sacramento Street), that the Decedent died three years ago, that they are concerned as to why the prices have been reduced, that they would like a report on each property within 30-days and come back on August 19th for a status report, and that they are requesting that the Trustee distribute some of the cash/do a partial distribution. Mr. Wolff stated that there is no basis to require a report on marketing, that no level of communication is enough for Majid, that they have provided information and he does not feel anything more will be provided in a report, that he thinks it is wrong to order distribution at this point and that the matter has not been briefed. The Court asked if it would be helpful to set a mediation deadline and a trial date and work backwards. Mr. Wolff stated that he is not sure mediation works in this case, that the properties are listed, that hopefully they will be sold, that there is no clear picture as to what the damage is and that he does not have any discovery to do on his end. Mr. Thirkell stated that he agrees that it is not appropriate to go to mediation at this point. The Court stated that it will not order a marketing report, but encourages the parties to provide it, and that they meet and confer and make all efforts to provide a report by July 22nd. Massoud requested immediate suspension, spoke of the Trustee's salary, the improvement projects, stated that the Trustee is selling the properties for less than what they were valued at two years ago and that the Trustee is negatively impacting the trust. Majid expressed his concerns as well. The Court stated that it is not prepared to suspend the Trustee, and that if at the next hearing there is no progress, it is appropriate for the Judicial Officer to set a trial date. The Petition to Compel Distributions to Beneficiaries, etc. and the pretrial conference are continued to August 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are to think of dates they would like to set for trial.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To 1. Compel Distributions To Beneficiaries 2. For Breach Of Trust And Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; 3. For Waste And To Compel Court Confirmatio
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Continued To Apr-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Mansour for and with Majid Farmanfarmaian (Petitioner).
Charles Wolff for and with Mansoor Farmanfarmaian (Trustee/Respondent).

Mr. Mansour stated that they received a status report, that the Sacramento property will be listed for sale on February 6th, and that the PG&E meters were installed in the Jones Street property, but they now have some issues with leaking in the two units. The Trustee stated that the meters were installed, that plumbing work needs to be done hopefully in two weeks, and that they can then rent the unit and then put it on the market. Mr. Wolff stated that it is difficult to file a petition for final distribution when they don't know how much there is to be distributed. The Trustee stated that there is about $1,000,000.00 held in reserve for the taxes, that his Mother did not pay the taxes, that she had foreign accounts, and that it is his understanding that the IRS will release any liability to him in April, but funds may be needed for any litigation. The Petition to Compel Distribution, etc. and the pre-trial conference are continued to April 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting petition that is on calendar for March 11, 2019 is also continued to April 8, 2019. Status on filing of a petition for preliminary distribution is also set for April 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Wolff for and with Mansoor Farmanfarmaian (Trustee/Petitioner).
Gordon Young for and with Majid Farmanfarmaian (Objecting Party).
Massoud Farmanfarmaian in Propria Persona.

Massoud and Mr. Young stated that they had no knowledge of the $15,000.00 that went to the Sister. All Counsel indicated that there is no issue regarding the windows. Discussion was held with regard to the accounting including distribution that was made to the Sister when paying off her mortgage, the accounting including anything else that was in the safe, whether or not it is appropriate to make a preliminary distribution to the Brothers, whether or not the potential estate tax and income tax liability should prevent preliminary distribution, sale of the properties paying for any tax liability, whether the tax issue can be addressed with a lien on the property, pro-ration of the taxes, when sale of the property can be expected, mediation, the parties having a right to see the properties, etc. Mansoor explained that there is minor work left to be done to the Sacramento Street property, that they are updating the three bedroom unit which will remain vacant and that the other unit will be rented to attract more buyers, that they are still waiting on PG&E to install the meters in the Jones Street property, that the timeline from PG&E to finish Jones Street is four to six weeks, that if the work is done there is a possibility of listing the property in mid January and raised the issue of unforeseen problems that may come up. The Court noted that the Sister is benefitting from receiving the property and that the other Beneficiaries are paying for the taxes on a property they do not share in and it isn't fair. Mr. Wolf stated that he will file something with the Court regarding pro-ration of the taxes. The Court directed Mr. Wolf to file a petition for preliminary distribution with a hearing date of April 1, 2019. The First Report and Account of Trustee is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Reporter: Kimberly D'Urso, CSR #11372, 530-521-2948, dursoreporting@gmail.com.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Continued To Oct-01-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Wolff for and with Mansoor Farmanfarmaian.
Gordon Young for and with Majid Farmanfarmaian.

The Court suggests a preliminary distribution, informed Counsel that he needs to file an amendment to the accounting taking out the Emeryville property and questions why the Trust paid for the Emeryville property when it was held in joint tenancy. Mr. Wolff stated that the mortgage on the property was an obligation of the Decedent. Mr. young stated that he believes the accounting can be ruled on, that some issues have been narrowed and some haven't, that this was designed to cover from sale of all of the Mom's jewelry, that it was done prematurely, that they did not file a notice of proposed action and that everybody understood that the Daughter was to receive the jewelry. Mr. Young also stated that this is a very serious breach of Trust, that the Trustee has made no distribution in two years, that the Court approved higher Trustee fees, that the Trustee informed them that he is unable to sell the property, and raised the issue of suspending the Trustee and appointing a neutral Fiduciary. Mr. Wolff stated that removal requires an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Young stated that he will submit something indicating what is left in the accounting, confirmed that the October 22nd date is not a discovery cut off and stated that 50% of the taxes should be paid by the Estate as the property was held in joint tenancy. Mr. Wolff stated that this is an issue to be addressed once the taxes are filed, that they are working very hard on selling the properties, that one is a six unit property on Jones Street, that they got it legalized, that it needs a metering box and there is an issue with the amount of electricity going into the property, that they are working with P G & E, that the other property is a soon to be two unit condominium on Sacramento Street, that there is an issue with the Home Owner's Association and the City with respect to the landscaping plans, that they are close to getting them to agree on the plans and that they can then get it sold. The Court suggests sending a letter to everyone and see what they want to do. Majid stated that not everyone agrees to sell the properties. The First Account and Report of Trustee is continued to October 1, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Young is to submit his report by September 4, 2018. Mr. Wolff is to file his response by September 17, 2018.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Reporter: Jennifer Roux, CSR #11033, 877-451-1580.

Appearances: Charles Wolff for and with Mansoor Farmanfarmaian (Trustee).
David Mansour for Majid Farmanfarmaian.
Massoud Farmanfarmaian in Propria Persona.

Mr. Mansour stated that he objected to the supplemental accounting. Mr. Wolff stated that he received the objection late on Thursday, and that he had no time to review it or to respond to it. Mr. Mansour stated that there were some problems with e-file and that he has no objection to the Court permitting additional time. With respect to the Trustees compensation, Mr. Wolff stated that he would like a start date of April 30, 2018 which is the original hearing date of the petition. Mr. Mansour stated that he does not agree with that and there is a mortgage issue that is not included in the petition. Massoud stated that he does not agree with the amount of fees for Mansoor (Trustee), that Mansoor has not met his responsibilities, that he does not have the Beneficiaries best interest, spoke of delay in sale of a property, delay in distribution that was due to him upon vacating the premises, etc. Mr. Wolff stated that he has a problem with the alleged failure to submit receipts, that the objection to supplemental accounting claims there are $58,000.00 in receipts that are missing, and that things would go a lot further if Counsel could provide information regarding what receipts/transactions are missing. The Court stated that it will not order something that is not before it. The Petition to Compel Distributions to Beneficiaries, etc. and the pre-trial conference are continued to October 22, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The First Report and Account of Trustee is continued to August 20, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Modification of Trustees Compensation, etc. is under submission.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2018 Continued To Jun-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Wolff for and with Mansoor Farmanfarmaian (Trustee)
Mary and Terrence Mayo for and with Majid Farmanfarmaian (Petitioner)

Mr. Wolff stated that he will file an accounting providing all of the bank statements, receipts, and check images within 45 days. Ms. Mayo agreed to pay for the check images, stated that some of the objections have been resolved, and that she will submit specific objections. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the petition for modification of Trustee's compensation being ruled on today, whether or not there is a necessity for the Trustee to continue working without being paid, whether he should be given authorization for payment of prospective fees going forward, having a professional fiduciary appointed, delay in administration of the Trust, etc. Ms. Mayo informed the Court that she has submitted a request for additional relief to prevent the Trustee from participating in litigation with the HOA at the Sacramento Street property, and that the Trustee provide written notice of sale for the Sacramento Street property and the Jones Street property. Mr. Wolff argued that he preferred not to be required to give notice with respect to the sale of the real properties, that a lot of these properties are going to be 1031 exchanges, that providing notice will provide time for objections and will take the potential 1031 buyers off the table. The First Report and Account of Trustee is continued to June 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of accounting to include bank statements, receipts, and check images. The Petition for Modification of Trustees Compensation, etc., is continued to May 30, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition to Compel Distributions/Remove Trustee, etc., is set for an Evidentiary Hearing (two half days) on August 31, 2018 and September 14, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Trial briefs, witness and exhibit lists are to be filed at least 10 days prior. A Pre-Trial Conference is set for August 27, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
